**NH REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**LABOR DAY WEEKEND EDITION**
|
---
**_A Note From Wayne_**
Dear Derek,
We are wrapping up another great week for Republicans in the Granite State.
The week started with a packed NHYR Lobster Bake in Londonderry on Saturday, showing that the Young Republicans are excited and eager to lead throughout this election cycle.
On Thursday night, the NHGOP held our Roast and Tribute to Governor John H. Sununu, with a room filled with over 300 committed Republicans. This event will be memorable for all of those who were in attendance.
As Granite Staters relax this long weekend, Republicans have our eye on the general election, which will begin in just over a week. [We announced our Unity Breakfast for Thursday, September 13th. Join us there!](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyEOO-2BkJ-2BsWAEbgSzNBg1MU0-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30M1eaKFHrqpoqQkHOu1eswhSHJcb4cV6LeqGwhNdI493eHAKUfkuob4tDUM6BZHqaNIC5LPfm6Q0CMC0mUud1wmwSGcrnVoQCw9-2B4Gfd-2B0ocqvdAhGJGqaFfFZ80NOJHY6J6CqwNMlVWa5Hwa4HDUnc0g62GiS9cKzRPtQluz1xu-2BGoDeSuHlX1MTA9LkKadfgDUkCJTq2FjrhOt8AAq0pyKhBdlbPbyfEMCvS1VvwKssvyFWxEa3fkOGlvj7TR-2FEWH87K28cULsKO3-2FcpxNI0vA-3D-3D)

Sincerely,
Wayne MacDonald

clean_body: | | nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER LABOR DAY WEEKEND EDITION | A Note From Wayne Dear Derek, We are wrapping up another great week for Republicans in the Granite State. The week started with a packed NHYR Lobster Bake in Londonderry on Saturday, showing that the Young Republicans are excited and eager to lead throughout this election cycle. On Thursday night, the NHGOP held our Roast and Tribute to Governor John H. Sununu, with a room filled with over 300 committed Republicans. This event will be memorable for all of those who were in attendance. As Granite Staters relax this long weekend, Republicans have our eye on the general election, which will begin in just over a week. Everything is on the line this November - get involved in our Party to help secure victories up and down the ballot. Sincerely, Wayne MacDonald Join the Grassroots Circle! The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor. Intern with the NH GOP! Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics? ** Learn the skills necessary to win elections and meet the Republican leaders that govern our State and write legislation. name: Tabitha Isner
subject: This holiday weekend brought to you by Organized Labor
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP01/1/62300/images/Logos/Logo3.PNG]

Dear Friend,

These days we don't notice how good we got it.

We complain endlessly about how busy we are, even as the average hours of work per week has gone down by 40% since the 19th century , meaning that we have FAR more time for leisure than ever before. Click here to see a graph: https://ourworldindata.org/working-hours

And - hallelujah! - work has gotten WAY safer. We're five times less likely to die at work as we were in 1928, and far less of us suffer significant health problems as a result of the work we do.

We've got weekends now and paid time off. And sick time and FMLA.

And all of this incredible progress has come because of Organized Labor - brave working folks who stood up to their bosses and demanded better working conditions. They risked their lives and their livelihoods. (When they protested in the streets, the police often killed protesters. Sound familiar?)

But they prevailed. And now, more than a hundred years later, we have a 40-hour work week, a long weekend in September, and so much more because of their dedication.

And the work hasn't stopped. Unions continue to push for policies that protect workers from capricious bosses and anti-family policies. They work for fair trade and for environmental justice. They work for health care and pensions and equal opportunity.

They work for all of us, whether or not we are members of a union. Throughout history, the work they do ends up helping ALL of us.

I, for one, will be celebrating this Labor Day by calling all my labor union friends and telling them how much it means to me that they haven't stopped fighting for workers rights.

Thank you for fighting for workers.
Now let me fight for you.

-Tabitha K. Isner
Endorsed by RWDSU, AL AFL-CIO, IBEW, and AFGE

Peter,

**As the American worker goes, so goes America**. This Labor Day, you will see messages like "Labor Unions: The Folks Who Gave You the Weekend." These reminders are important, and hopefully serve as a call to action. As an [article in the Washington Post](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/2080687/10804703/-990678157?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOTU2YjlmMi03OWFmLWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMzY2YzEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jGB98DfnhaTqsq9a_pYGZNGPsdD13P8GYFFjRZGmv8A=&utm_term=.0d194202fe41) reminds us, **labor unions have been a key force in U.S. history in the fight against wealth inequality.** **As people of conscience, we are fighting to protect our democracy from unprecedented attacks. **We are defending our voting rights, our free press, our protections against discrimination, our right to organize in the workplace, our federal courts, our safety net. In almost every instance, the folks who are funding these attacks are among the wealthiest of our society, willing to do anything to preserve their power. [**Mike Siegel is fighting for healthcare for all, a living wage, and a retirement with dignity. Chip in $20 today to send him to Congress.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/2080688/10804704/-2058851995?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOTU2YjlmMi03OWFmLWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMzY2YzEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jGB98DfnhaTqsq9a_pYGZNGPsdD13P8GYFFjRZGmv8A=) **The American Dream is fading, and it will take working people joining together to bring it back.** When the labor movement was at its strongest, the American economy was at its most fair. When workers are allowed to join together to advocate for better pay and benefits, healthier working conditions, and real people's representatives, the entire country benefits. **At the top of my agenda is repealing the 2017 tax cuts, because they set the wrong priority for this country.** We know that "trickle-down economics" is a fraud. If we want to rebuilt our economy, we need to invest in the American people, which is why my platform includes:
* **Medicare for All** – to ensure that every American can access high quality care, regardless of wealth or pre-existing conditions.
* **A living wage** – because no one should have to work two or three jobs to support a family.
* **A retirement with dignity** – we must strengthen Social Security to keep our seniors out of poverty.
* **Massive infrastructure investment** – to put Americans back to work restoring our bridges and roads, strengthening our flood control infrastructure, and bringing high speed Internet to every rural county.

**This Labor Day, let's recommit ourselves to fighting for a fair economy for all**. As a union member myself, I am honored to have the support of the Texas AFL-CIO, the Austin and Houston labor councils, and numerous individual unions. Together, we can win not only this race, but elections across the country, and begin to put America back on track. **[Contribute today to elect a Congressman who will fight for working people.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/2080689/10804705/-2058851995?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOTU2YjlmMi03OWFmLWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMzY2YzEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jGB98DfnhaTqsq9a_pYGZNGPsdD13P8GYFFjRZGmv8A=)** And please enjoy a safe and happy Labor Day, y'all! In solidarity,
Mike

name: Belinda Biafore
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by the WV State Democratic Executive Committee (www
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP02/1/67825/images/D%20Logo.png]

Fellow Democrat,

I can't say enough to truly explain what our brothers and sisters of labor mean to us. Unions are the backbone of America's workforce and they fought hard, gave their bodies and even their lives to ensure the protections, wages, and benefits we take advantage of today.

Today, we celebrate and honor our brothers and sisters of labor, but we also have to stress the importance of the fight we're putting up against constant attacks on our jobs, our workers and our families from Republican Leaders in West Virginia and America.

We vow today, and every day, to fight alongside West Virginia workers and their families to ensure that their jobs, workplace protections, and wages and benefits are not taken from them.

Just as our teachers and school service personnel did as they stood up to the Republican Party's attacks this past Legislative Session, we fight even harder when they want us to give up.

Republican Leaders and their big money corporations only care about themselves and not workers. They take our healthcare, our jobs, and our livelihoods.

West Virginia workers work hard and today we celebrate them as we honor our friends of labor and vow to keep up the fight!

Thank you to all of our brothers and sisters. We will continue to stand with you every step of the way.

Sincerely,

Belinda Biafore, Chair

body:
| | Hey -

Wanted to write you a short note today about Labor Day. I'm down in Buena Vista getting ready to hit a community breakfast at Parry McCluer High School and then walk in the Buena Vista Labor Day Parade, and later today Anne and I will join friends for an annual Labor Day cookout. But before I do all that, I wanted to take a second to acknowledge why we have this day "off," and what we're celebrating.

Labor unions in Virginia and all across America are essential to protecting the rights of our workers across industries. The labor movement is part of our American success story - we band together to have one another's backs, and through that togetherness, we can create a rising tide that lifts all boats.

[If you're with me - proud to support workers and labor unions - add your name today to say so!](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1115/3e0906/6d17ddfe/3e36e7d8/2902985052/VEsH/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/Always-Stand-With-Workers?source=20180903_1_PET_TK_Labor-Day&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+If+youre+with+me++proud+to+support+worke&utm_campaign=20180903_1_PET_TK_Labor-Day")

Thanks - and happy Labor Day!

_Tim_

name: Steve Sisolak
subject: Labor Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY FRIENDS FOR STEVE SISOLAK
body:
---
Dear Friend,

Labor Day isn't just about getting a day off from work or having a cookout.

It's about remembering the contributions that workers and their unions have made to our country, our state, and our way of life -- and committing to equal, fair pay for equal work and to protecting benefits and safe conditions for all working people.

The labor movement has made life better for Americans throughout our history -- and here in Nevada service employees, builders, and other workers are still driving our economy to this day.

As governor, I'll be committed to defending the progress we've made and standing up for working people, because they've earned it.

So today, join me in thanking working men and women in Nevada and across the country.

Thanks,

Steve

name: Jason Yates
subject: Labor for God - in everything you do.
body:
| | Today we celebrate the American workforce
---
| Peter, Working can be an expression of faith as we use our God-given talents to make a difference in a broken world. Today we honor hard-working Americans of every trade. Rev. Martin Luther King Jr. said it best: **“If a man is called to be a street sweeper, he should sweep streets even as a Michelangelo painted, or Beethoven composed music or Shakespeare wrote poetry. He should sweep streets so well that all the hosts of heaven and earth will pause to say, ‘Here lived a great street sweeper who did his job well.’”** In ALL we DO, we have an opportunity to work unto the Lord (Colossians 23:24). “No work is insignificant. All labor that uplifts humanity has dignity and importance and should be undertaken with painstaking excellence.”
-Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. We can tend to generalize characters in the Bible as people “in ministry” without remembering that you can be “in ministry” as a carpenter (Jesus), tentmaker (Paul) or fisherman (Peter). God has called each of us to different jobs, but our purpose to serve Him in our work remains the same. “There are different kinds of gifts, but the same Spirit distributes them. There are different kinds of service, but the same Lord. **There are different kinds of working, but in all of them and in everyone it is the same God at work.** ”
(1 Corinthians 12:4-6 NIV) As we serve the Lord in our vocations, we also have the opportunity to serve and bring glory to God in our communities and in our nation. God placed you in your community, to be His ambassador, to bring biblical truth and principles to light. But are you making the most of—or simply taking for granted—this God-given opportunity? **When our faith is turned to action through our votes, God will be honored in the public square and Christian principles advanced for the good of America and the world!** Will you commit to stand for Biblical values at the voting booth?
* **If so, please take a minute to sign-up for your free****[Personal Voting Assistant](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=qQ_RPm-AZQZC4VpsXuK6dYN2PX-ctnCdm7TNagsjaA8rNhflQUW0zqSR8jlco1El&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180903com01&n=1).** It’s our mission to see 90-million Americans stand united at the polls—beginning with you. The Personal Voting Assistant makes it easy for Christians to get ready to vote and never miss a single election.
* If you’re already signed up, share the **Personal Voting Assistant** with 5 of your friends or family today!

Together, let’s seek to serve the Lord in all we do—including at the voting booth! [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=qQ_RPm-AZQZC4VpsXuK6dYN2PX-ctnCdm7TNagsjaA8rNhflQUW0zqSR8jlco1El&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180903com01&n=2) **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO

name: Ben Jealous
subject: Labor unions lifted my family out of poverty
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
What today is truly about.

| [](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37bcc0/7ab0e2c4/7e29c35a/3498b39f/1724268451/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-fund-fight?refcode=OHQ09038A") Friend, Today marks the end of a long weekend -- and unfortunately, the traditional end of summer. But who can you thank for this long weekend, and, in fact, every weekend? Labor unions. Today is Labor Day, and before it became about hot dogs, family picnics, and one last visit to the pool, it was created to celebrate the social and economic achievements of the American worker. As a former union member of the Communications Workers of America, and the grandson of an American Federation of State, County, and Municipal Employees (AFSCME) family, I know the power that unions have to transform lives and families. In fact, the day we walked out of poverty was the day our family became a two union household. Both my grandparents belonged to AFSCME, and it was their union pensions that put me through college. It's why I joined the union movement so young -- and it's why I know we can be doing more. In this time when the president, the Supreme Court, and Congress are attacking unions, our states have to stand up for them. We should be using project labor agreements and encouraging young people to find a trade and a union that supports them in it. Careers like these should not be found through luck, but through support that begins as early as elementary school. I'm proud to have the endorsement of strong unions, including the Maryland and DC chapters of the AFL-CIO, AFSCME Council 3, Baltimore Teachers Union, Maryland Postal Workers Union, CWA, SEIU, UNITE HERE! Local 7, UFCW 27, UFCW 400, UFCW 1994, ATU 1300, Amalgamated Transit Union International, and countless other organizations and activists fighting for workers in Maryland. And I will keep fighting every day for a stronger economy built with labor unions and working families. Thanks, Ben | [CHIP IN »](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37bcc0/7ab0e2c4/7e29c35a/3498b39f/1724268451/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-fund-fight?refcode=OHQ09038A")

Stop this nominee from joining the Supreme Court now. | | | [](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/Bdyaj/6/t6/r5cs/61bbvl/zXOB82HMI4/P/P) |

| | Activist,

**Every right that we hold dear is on the line. Every freedom that we’ve fought for is at stake.**

name: Josh Hawley
subject: My latest OpEd: A new way to give hardworking Missourians what they deserve
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Hawley for Senate[Click here to unsubscribe
body:
I wanted to make sure you saw my latest OpEd in the Kansas City Star Hello Peter, I wanted to make sure you saw my latest op-ed that was published in the [Kansas City Star](https://email.joshhawley.co/u/click?_t=11a592fdc01846d4a1c728421295853d&_m=e331f00339f6498b9e16658e0210d725&_e=lPW15VxHxdzn3g3F-fVFCSPBpCO8eMy9myEQF7PUu5S50A08QlyxCWSnWN0m0qDDw_rwsyoQOA0_Dlrj9d_H7gmHUwL09UAzCboEbNMy2wAMWS3JiSmwCM9cmEsgesm9PDCWcHQY4HCQGAciTAWn0Ipivyi-lpbRjqfCYw7KssgEfSswfqmGEcALy3RvCYDCESmQ2ib-BArD91bTlAsRYb4-6sqq2Sv8SxKwI52TBwGBdIpU61b2DQP8rhMKRy6X6mL06jYvt0lwxG0ssQLDNSGzubX7aCRAAo4xlyUpCVc6QPUnM75HCMS1HwgoRRZuKB4CmGlVGgadgJzGB0T-kg%3D%3D). For too long, our economy has failed to reward hard work while politicians like Senator McCaskill have grown rich off taxpayer money. Call me old-fashioned, but I believe Missouri deserves a senator who will put workers and working families ahead of self-advancement, someone who will fight for an economy where working people get the respect and reward they deserve. To get there, we’ve got to start thinking differently. Thank you for your time. I hope you and your family have a fun and safe Labor Day. For Missouri, Josh Hawley BY JOSH HAWLEY
Special to The Star

Growing up, if there was one thing my family valued above just about everything, it was hard work. I learned it on my grandpa’s farm, cutting weeds and changing gates in his fields in the summer. I saw it modeled by my parents, who never missed a day of work that I can recall. And I’ve witnessed it all over our state, in the men and women who rise before dawn to head to the job site or out to the field. Hard work is a way of life for most Missourians.

But for too long now, our economy hasn’t rewarded work like it should. And it hasn’t rewarded workers. Instead, the government has paid people not to work and has left working families to fend for themselves.

You can thank the Washington politicians for that, beginning with Sen. Claire McCaskill. McCaskill has spent 36 long years in politics, the last 12 in Washington, and what does she have to show for it? Jobs lost overseas. Working wages flat. Trade deals that benefit our competitors at the cost of our own people. Meanwhile, she’s gotten rich off taxpayer money, raking in more than $130 million in government subsidies for her family business. Now she’s one of the richest members of Congress. Shocking — but so typical of Washington.

Call me old-fashioned, but I believe Missouri deserves a senator who will put workers and working families ahead of self-advancement, someone who will fight for an economy where working people get the respect and reward they deserve. To get there, we’ve got to start thinking differently.

Start by putting workers first in our trade policy. For decades now, working wages have been held flat by unfair deals that encourage taking jobs from Americans and giving them to cheap labor overseas. We need deals that prioritize the skills and effort of American workers. President Donald Trump’s recent trade agreement with Mexico is a good start.

We also need to invest in our workers, in their skills and education. Four-year colleges have had a monopoly on public dollars for too long, even as they charge more and turn out increasingly worthless degrees. It’s time to allow workers to use federal student loan money for apprenticeship programs and job-training courses. Break up the college monopoly. It shouldn’t be that you have to get an expensive four-year degree to get a job — or respect — in this country.

We need to put vocational training back into high schools. As part of that effort, we should explore partnerships with private employers that would offer a high school junior or senior the chance to take classes in the morning and learn a skill in the afternoon, with the goal of graduating ready for a job.

And we need to start rewarding work rather than welfare. The federal government spends billions every year on various and duplicative programs that pay people not to work. That money can be put to better use. We should consolidate those programs and tighten eligibility requirements so that money flows only to those truly in need. And we should use the savings to invest directly in our workers.

I support a wage increase for every American worker who makes below the median wage. Call it a work credit. If you make less than the median wage, this credit would boost your per-hour wage to bring it 50 percent closer to the median. And the credit would come to you in every paycheck.

We already have something like this in the form of the EITC — the Earned Income Tax Credit. But the EITC is too complicated, too hard to administer and too prone to abuse. We should overhaul the EITC and convert it into a true work credit, to be delivered through the payroll tax system to every American who works a steady job. American workers deserve a return on the work they do that reflects its true value to our country. And they deserve support against the cheap labor and anti-competitive practices of other countries overseas.

Washington has given us an economy that works brilliantly for earners at the top, but provides fewer and fewer opportunities for everyone else. While people like McCaskill get rich, working people watch their futures fade away. We can do better than that. And we can start by sending someone to Washington who hasn’t been part of their bickering and failure, someone ready to lead a new fight for Missouri workers. STAND WITH JOSH - DONATE TODAY

name: Bob Menendez
subject: To our union brothers and sisters
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Menendez for Senate
body:
Friend,

As we spend today with family and friends, enjoying the final days of summer - think back to the great achievements that our union brothers and sisters have accomplished in our state's history.

From increasing the minimum wage to protecting collective bargaining rights to providing paid sick leave, New Jersey workers and unions have a proud legacy of moving the Garden State forward.

I am proud to work alongside incredible leaders from across our state to ensure that families and workers are protected and that the valuable work they do each and every day is recognized.

So to the police officers, firefighters, construction workers, teachers, public employees, and other workers who do so much for our state - I just want to say thank you.

Happy Labor Day,

Bob

name: Tom Wolf
subject: Happy Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tom Wolf for Governor
body:
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | Hi there,

I've always believed that a vibrant, healthy community requires good-paying jobs and a thriving, secure middle class. And that the middle class is the foundation for the success of our Commonwealth.

That's why today, I've been reflecting on all the incredible things working people have done to build our state.

As a former business owner, I know that if you listen to workers and treat them right with living wages, good benefits, and safe working conditions, we all benefit. That's why my former company shared 20-30% of its profits with the workers who help make the business successful.

As governor, I'm working to empower working people and create jobs that pay. I've signed an executive order that ensures employees and contractors working for state agencies will be paid no less than $12.00 an hour and I've called on the state Legislature to raise the wage across the commonwealth. And I've modernized outdated overtime rules to help strengthen the middle class and provide fairness to workers.

There's so much work ahead of us, which is why I'm glad to have you on my team.

I hope you enjoy your Labor Day, and spend some time reflecting on all the incredible things working people have done to build our state. Together, we can rebuild a thriving, secure middle class across Pennsylvania.

Thank you,

Tom Wolf
---

name: Joe Donnelly
subject: Happy Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
The labor movement has always had one simple mission: to advocate for and protect workers. And through the millions of people in it and behind it, this movement has fought for fair hours, better wages |
---

| | Peter,

Today, we celebrate working men and women and all they have contributed to make our state and country a better place to work and live.

The labor movement has always had one simple mission: to advocate for and protect workers. And through the millions of people in it and behind it, this movement has fought for fair hours, better wages, and safer working conditions -- changing the lives of all Americans.

I've seen firsthand how much good the labor movement has done for our state, but I know we have so much to keep fighting for.

I'm spending today traveling from town to town to celebrate the grassroots power and successes of the labor movement, but also to hear from hard-working Hoosiers about what's working for them, and what's not. I'll take those ideas and concerns back to the Senate because the men and women who go to work in the dark and come home in the dark to build a better life for themselves and their families deserve a senator who will protect their jobs and livelihoods.

So on this Labor Day, I send my deepest gratitude to all the workers who've come before, all the workers with us now, and all the workers who will shape this country for generations to come.

Thank you all, and happy Labor Day.

Joe |
---|---|---

Friends, This Labor Day, I want to take a moment to recognize Arizona's working families, and to celebrate the efforts of America's labor unions to build this country's middle class and create an economy that works for all of us. **For generations, American workers have organized to make sure our workplaces are safe and welcoming environments for everyone.** They fought tirelessly for better wages and for retirement security, and workers in Arizona and across the country are better off today because of their efforts. As your congressman, I am proud to stand with Arizona's working families, today and every day, and I promise to continue to stand with you. Together, we will fight to protect and strengthen critical earned benefits programs, including Social Security and Medicare, and we will create more good-paying jobs in our communities. Yours, Tom P.S. I promise to keep up the fight for Arizona's working families in Congress, but I'm in a tough race for re-election against a far-right opponent. | | | Friend, Every day across Georgia, hardworking people go to work to make better lives for themselves, their families, and their communities. Labor Day is an opportunity for our nation to stand in solidarity with working people of all stripes - to reflect on their hard fought victories and the fights yet to be won. These fights - for dignity, fair wages, and affordable health care - are personal to me. After three years of being a member of retail workers union RWDSU Local 3, and feeling the direct impact that public policy had on the lives of everyday working people, I became an organizer for the Working Families Party to fight for workers' rights. I joined this campaign because Leader Abrams has a track record of standing up for working families. In the Georgia House, Abrams led the fight against wage theft when no one else would. She's been a strong supporter of paid sick leave legislation, project labor agreements, and closing the gender wage gap. I know Stacey Abrams will continue these fights to build a Georgia where wages earned are wages received, and where every family can thrive. **[Today marks 64 days until Election Day. Although many of us have the day off, we can't stop working if we want to elect a champion for working people. Will you chip in to help elect Stacey Abrams our next governor?](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa9e1/34a064cc/c2c6478/70e36f0/138663069/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUwOTAzMTgtZnVsbCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-september?refcode=IE090318-full")** There's a reason why Stacey Abrams has earned the support of Georgia workers and more than a dozen unions. We can trust Leader Abrams to not only stand in solidarity with working people, but lead in the fight for wages workers can raise a family on, stopping misclassification and wage theft and more. Which is why we have to do the work to elect Leader Abrams as the next Governor of Georgia. **So please, make a contribution to elect Stacey Abrams - the champion working families in Georgia need to fight by their side:** **[https://abrams.bsd.net/StandWithWorkers](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa9e1/34a064cc/c2c6478/70e36f0/138663069/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUwOTAzMTgtZnVsbCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-september?refcode=IE090318-full")** When working families thrive, Georgia thrives. Happy Labor Day! Dom Leon-Davis

name: J.D. Scholten
subject: A Critical Fight for Labor
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee
body:
_Last week I attended a public hearing on the proposed UI Labor Center._

Peter,
Today we celebrate the American worker, and particularly the role unions and organized labor has played in building this country and our middle class. It’s no secret that as union membership declines, so does the middle class. For decades, people in power and corporate CEO’s have chipped away at unions rights and the ability for workers to collectively bargain.
Here in Iowa, our labor unions have faced a number of attacks from state government officials over the last two years, and now **the University of Iowa has shockingly announced plans to close the UI Labor Center**. Although this is technically a state issue, it’s part of a nationwide trend to strip our workers of collective bargaining rights. As a strong supporter of unions, I feel a responsibility to share this story, and to ask you to do what you can to help.
**Here are the facts (via[saveourlaborcenter.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/2086524/10833836/-1434885807?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOTk1MWEzYS1kNmFmLWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMzY2YzEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jEN4xltK5RT4iRQC7Lt493QZ8kxGOTKKGKFHemXEZs0=)):**
* The UI Labor Center — the only unit in the entire state university system that specializes in research and education for and about Iowa workers.

* The decision to close the Center was made with NO prior discussion with the Labor Center, UI faculty, workers, students, or community partners who rely on the Center’s education and research.

* The Center was established in 1951 and has been built by the contributions and support of generations of Iowans for nearly 70 years.

* The Center provides direct education for more than 2,500 Iowa workers annually across the state. It impacts many thousands more as these continuing education students bring knowledge of health and safety laws, anti-discrimination rights, and leadership skills back to their workplaces and communities to grow Iowa’s economy.

* Faculty who study and teach on labor and employment law, immigration, public policy, Iowa history, occupational health and safety, and the economy rely on the Center as a resource and hub for coordinating interdisciplinary research on a wide range of work-related issues. Center staff teach undergraduate classes, mentor students, supervise experiential student learning, research projects, and internships, and support student job placement in labor and employment-related fields. These functions are not duplicated anywhere else in the Regents system.

* The work of the Center is sustained by funds from program fees, competitive grants, and a small but essential University funding commitment.

**There are three steps you can take to help:**
* **SIGN THE PETITION:[CLICK HERE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/2086525/10833837/1090339165?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOTk1MWEzYS1kNmFmLWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMzY2YzEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jEN4xltK5RT4iRQC7Lt493QZ8kxGOTKKGKFHemXEZs0=)** to show your support with this 30 second action!

* **CALL AND WRITE:[CLICK HERE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/2086526/10833838/2064246499?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOTk1MWEzYS1kNmFmLWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMzY2YzEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jEN4xltK5RT4iRQC7Lt493QZ8kxGOTKKGKFHemXEZs0=)** to voice your opinion to decision makers at the University of Iowa via email or phone!

* **SPREAD THE WORD:** Forward this email or post about the closure on social media to share with your friends and neighbors!

An attack on collective bargaining and organized labor is an attack on all of us in the working class, which is why I’m asking you to join me in this critical fight.
Thank you.
Standing Tall and in Solidarity for All,
J.D. Scholten
